_step: 1. (client) declare tools — usually in session.update; shown here on a response.create for clarity

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city.

_step: 2. (client) user turn (text example; with audio+VAD this happens automatically)
3. (client) ask the model to respond — ONE response.create per turn
6. (server) the response that wants a tool — full data also lands here
7. (client) YOU execute get_weather(), then return the result by call_id
8. (client) ask the model to speak the answer — the SECOND response.create is a NEW turn, not a duplicate of step 3

_note: Invariant: exactly one response.create per turn. A choice/tool result is a NEW user turn. With semantic/server VAD, set turn_detection.create_response=false to own these fires and avoid the VAD auto-response double-fire. There is NO native realtime web_search tool — implement web_search as a client function whose handler calls the Responses API (gpt-5/gpt-4o with web_search) and returns function_call_output.
